summary: Tight first set, soaring Space, mixed second‑set flow
blurb: The show opens with a lively first set featuring a laughing Jerry on Mississippi Half-Step Uptown Toodeloo and tight renditions of Candyman and China Cat Sunflower > I Know You Rider. Highlights include a scorching All Along The Watchtower and an expansive Space that showcases the drums as a giant chrome motor. The second set starts strong but loses focus toward the end, with sloppy moments on Louie Louie and Sugar Magnolia.
review:
April 22, 1988 at Irvine Meadows captures the Dead fresh off their East‑Coast run, firing up a first set that feels both tight and high‑energy. Jerry is in fine form – his laugh at the start of Mississippi Half-Step Uptown Toodeloo sets a relaxed tone, and his shredding solos on Watchtower and China Cat > Rider draw cheers throughout. Brent adds synth‑drummed textures during Space, while Mickey and Billy drive the band like a “giant chrome motor,” especially in the Drums > Space segment.
The second set begins with the classic China Cat Sunflower > I Know You Rider pairing and a powerful Estimated Prophet into He’s Gone, but momentum slips as the night wears on. Louie Louie gets a fiery cover, yet later jams feel unfocused, and Sugar Magnolia ends on a sloppy note. The encore closes with a heartfelt Black Muddy River that lifts the show back up. Overall, the performance shines in its early moments and standout songs, though the latter half shows signs of fatigue.

rating_calculation.sentiment_rationale: Two of the four analyses are negative, one is mixed and only one is positive. Reviewers praise the first set and several standout songs, but note a sloppy second‑set and lack of detailed musical commentary in two recordings, leading to an overall slightly negative sentiment.

best_recording.reason: Audience tape with good clarity that captures the high‑energy first set and standout songs, offering the most complete and vivid listening experience despite some second‑set slip‑ups.

key_highlights: High‑energy first set with tight playing and a laughing Jerry on Half‑Step
Scorching All Along The Watchtower and soaring Space featuring massive drum work
Strong China Cat Sunflower > I Know You Rider sequence, but second set loses focus toward the end

band_performance.Jerry: Shredding solos and solid tone on Watchtower; laughs into Half‑Step, but occasional sloppiness appears in later jams.
band_performance.Brent: Synth‑drummed textures during Space add an extra layer of atmosphere.
band_performance.Mickey: Drums described as a giant chrome motor that drives the band, especially on Drums > Space.
band_performance.Billy: Shares the powerful, motor‑like drive with Mickey, propelling the group through high‑energy sections.
